title: Syllabus
content:
Syllabus

Syllabus: FNCE 3010 - Corporate Finance

 

**See all 3 tabs for all syllabus information**

  •   Instructor and About the Course
  •   Assignments and Grading
  •   Course Policies and University Policies

Contact Information

Name: Bob Donchez

Email: Robert.Donchez@colorado.edu

Phone: 303-492-5916

Office Location: Koelbel 461

Office Hours: TTh 9:15 - 10:45 a.m. and by appointment

Communication

Must be conducted in person or via-email thru CU accredited account.

Course Description

Covers the theory and practices governing the management of capital in a business firm. Examines the determinants of capital requirements, methods of obtaining capital, problems of internal financial management, implications of risk, and methods of financial analysis and valuations. 

Course Objectives

After completing the course, students should possess:

  • A detailed understanding of the concepts & principles of financial managment, with particular emphasis on risk, rates of return, valuation.
  • An analytical approach to modern financial problem solving, including the use of financial tools.
  • An analytical knowledge, including modeling abilities, of capital projects and financing decisions.
  • An understanding and the applications of the interrelationships between business management and the entity’s financial statements, and the use of ratio analysis as an integrated tool.
  • A comprehensive ability to understand, derive, and determine valuations of (1) financial assets, including debt and equity instruments, (2) publicly-traded companies, (3) capital projects.
  • An advanced ability to understand and apply TVM calculations over a broad spectrum of financial issues and problems.
  • Written and oral presentation skills, with focus on technical quantitative abilities.
  • Advance Excel skills.

Prerequisites

  • BASE 2104

Required Texts

  • Brigham, and Ehrhardt, Financial Management: Theory and Practice, 17th Edition (2024)  Cengage Learning with Mind Tap on-line access. Available thru Cengage Unlimited, 1 term (4 months) Instant Access.

Mind Tap Registration: Make sure to access correct section as per below

Textbook: by Brigham & Ehrhardt “Financial Management: Theory & Practice 17e”

    • Course Title: FNCE 3010-005 Fall 2025 (8:00 a.m.)
    • Course Start Date: 8/21/25
  •  
    • Course Title: FNCE 3010-007 Fall 2025 (11:00 a.m.)
    • Course Start Date: 8/21/25
    • Course Title: FNCE 3010-010 Fall 2025 (2:00 p.m.)
    • Course Start Date: 8/21/25

Assignments

Finance is an applied discipline. In the real business world, both the “how to” and the “why” are important. The problems, questions and exercises listed on the following page represent the minimum level of work to be completed in order to enhance understanding of the course material. Check figures are available in the appendix and some may be reviewed in class. Additional problem sets will be distributed in class. If experiencing trouble working any of the assigned problems, questions or exercises, please visit during office hours. Please come prepared with specifics on the assignment issues, including all attempted efforts of the solutions. All readings should be completed prior to the class in which that material is covered. Homework due dates are when each listed assignment is due for collection and grading.  All deadlines must be met.

DELIVERABLES & ACCOUNTABILITY
Class Homework and Quizzes

Hard copy will be submitted in class. 

Online Quizzes and Excel Assignments

Completed in Mind Tap thru the Canvas course site.

Business Communication / Attendance/ Participation

In the business world, one must show up each day to work.  The same applies to this business course.  One must have strong business communications skills when interviewing and on the job.  This will also be a focus in the course, and includes class responses, answers, participation, e-mails, career appointments, attendance, and student conduct. Students should be able to provide homework solutions when called upon, and present cognizant replies,comments, and answers to questions in our class meetings, just as would be expected in business meetings in the real world.

 

Submission Policies

  • Textbook Assignment Problems & Questions:  Hard copy brought to class each day ready for submission.
  • Handout Problem Sets: Hard copy brought to class each day ready for submission.
  • MindTap On-line Quizzes: Completed and submitted on-line in MindTap thru Canvas.
  • MindTap On-line Excel Assignments: Completed and submitted on-line in MindTap thru Canvas.
  • In the Business world, all deadlines must be met.Missing deadlines on Wall Street costs real $ and business.  Likewise, all deadlines must be met, whether present or not. NO Exceptions.

Grading Criteria and Grade Weight Breakdown

Table 1: Grade Weight Breakdown
Activity Grade Weight
Exams (3) 66%
Class Homework & Quizzes 9%
Online Quizzes 8%
Business Comm./ Attendance/ Participation 9%
Online Excel Assignments          8% 
Total 100%
Exams

There are three exams each worth 22% of the course grade.

Improvement Factor

An improvement factor will be taken into consideration with the exams, which can alter the weights of each exam. If, for example, a student scores 60 %, on the first exam, then improves to 70 % on the second, the weight of the first score drops 2% from 22% to 20% while the weight of the improved second exam is bumped from 22% to 24%. If the third exam results improve (to, say 80% in the example) the third exam’s weight becomes 24%, while the first exam’s weight drops from 20% to 18%.

 

SYLLABUS: COURSE SCHEDULE Download SYLLABUS: COURSE SCHEDULE

Administration

All University of Colorado and Leeds School of Business rules, policies and procedures are followed in the course (including those related to grading, drop/add requirements and the grade of “IF). University policies regarding academic dishonesty and classroom conduct are followed. Please review the University of Colorado Student Honor Code and all other relevant University Policies.

Students with disabilities who qualify for academic accommodations must provide a letter from Disability Services (DS) and discuss specific needs with the professor, during the first two weeks of class. It is the student's responsibility to follow through with the professor regarding all DS accommodations for the course.  DS determines accommodations based on documented disabilities. Disabilities Services: 303-492-8671

Honor Code

Students are to sign and adhere to the honor code, available on the University web site and on plaques throughout business classrooms. The code states: "On my honor, as a University of Colorado student, I have neither given nor received unauthorized assistance on this work."

Business Expectations

Business courses prepare the student for the business world. In the real business world, one is required to show-up at work every day, on-time, ready and prepared to work. Therefore, the student is required to attend class every day, prepared and ready to discuss the relevant material. In the real world, business meetings are seldom a monologue; rather there is a dialog among the attendees. Likewise, students must be prepared to participate by reading the relevant material the night prior to its discussion in class, and by attempting any assigned problems and questions.

  • Cell phones ringing during business meetings are the quickest way to lose a client’s business. It’s an unprofessional practice and not tolerated during business meetings. Likewise, cell phones are not permitted in class. Cell phones must be disarmed prior to entering the meeting room.

Business Conduct/Expectations Policies Download Business Conduct/Expectations Policies

University Policies

You can find a copy of the University Policies under the CU Resources page.
